title: cymbopogon confertiflorus
intro: Cymbopogon confertiflorus, also known as East Indian lemongrass or Makah, is a type of grass native to tropical Asia, belonging to the same family as lemongrass and citronella. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Cymbopogon confertiflorus include reducing stress and anxiety, alleviating digestive issues, and relieving symptoms of colds and flu, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Cymbopogon confertiflorus can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, essential oils, and infusions, which can be consumed or used topically to harness its therapeutic benefits.
